plan_name: Phase A: Sequential Consumer Operations (Phase 5 Single-Pass)
description: Replace two-pass consumer ops (all merges, then all filters) in Phase 5 with a single pass that applies operations in YAML declaration order. This is ONE PART of fixing the reported bug — Phase B (repo-in-sequence resolution) is also required for the full fix.
blocked_on: context/phase-0-spec-discovery.json - spec decisions may revise task acceptance criteria (especially Q5: merge source removal behavior)

context.core_change: Replace two-pass execution with single sequential pass
out_of_scope: Allium spec updates (formalizing sequential execution in spec/common-repo.allium)

session_startup: Run git status to check branch (should be feat/sequential-operations-*)
Run ./script/test in background to verify baseline
Read this file to find current task
Find first task where status=pending and blocked_by=null

tasks.name: Write the key failing test: exclude-before-merge
tasks.description: Write the test that proves the bug exists. With exclude before merge in declaration order, sequential execution should cause the merge to error because the exclude removed the source file. With the current two-pass code, the merge runs first and succeeds.
steps: Open src/phases/local_merge.rs
Add test `test_phase5_sequential_exclude_before_merge_removes_source` to the `mod tests` block
Config: [Exclude fragment.json, Json merge fragment→output]
Assert: execute() returns Err (because exclude removed the merge source)
Run: cargo nextest run test_phase5_sequential_exclude_before_merge_removes_source
Expected: FAIL (current code runs merges first, so merge succeeds and Result is Ok)
acceptance_criteria: Test exists in src/phases/local_merge.rs mod tests block
Test fails with current implementation (proves bug exists)
Test uses TempDir, Operation::Exclude, Operation::Json, execute()
See docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-12-sequential-operations.md Task 2 for exact test code

tasks.name: Implement apply_consumer_operations single sequential pass
tasks.description: Replace apply_consumer_merges() and apply_consumer_filters() with a single apply_consumer_operations() that iterates the config once and dispatches each operation as it appears.
steps: Add new function apply_consumer_operations(final_fs, local_config) to src/phases/local_merge.rs
Function iterates local_config once with match on Operation enum
Match arms: Exclude, Include, Rename (filters) + Yaml, Json, Toml, Ini, Markdown, Xml (merges)
Update execute() function: replace the two calls (apply_consumer_merges + apply_consumer_filters) with single call to apply_consumer_operations
Delete old apply_consumer_merges() function (lines 211-236)
Delete old apply_consumer_filters() function (lines 242-262)
Run: cargo nextest run test_phase5_sequential_exclude_before_merge_removes_source
Expected: PASS (merge now errors because source was removed by preceding exclude)
acceptance_criteria: New apply_consumer_operations function exists with single for-loop over local_config
execute() calls apply_consumer_operations instead of the two old functions
Old apply_consumer_merges and apply_consumer_filters are removed

tasks.name: Update test_phase5_consumer_merges_before_filters for new semantics
tasks.description: The existing test asserts the OLD behavior (merges always before filters regardless of declaration order). Rename and update it to assert the NEW declaration-order behavior.
steps: Open src/phases/local_merge.rs
Find test_phase5_consumer_merges_before_filters (currently around line 426)
Rename to test_phase5_consumer_operations_run_in_declaration_order
Reorder config: merge FIRST, then exclude (matches new sequential model)
Assertions unchanged: merge produces merged output.json, then exclude removes fragment.json
Run: cargo nextest run test_phase5_consumer_operations_run_in_declaration_order
Expected: PASS
acceptance_criteria: Test renamed to describe new semantics
Config has merge before exclude (reversed from old test)

tasks.name: Run full unit test suite, verify no regressions
tasks.description: Ensure the refactor didn't break any existing tests.
steps: Run: cargo nextest run
If any test fails, diagnose and fix (likely other tests depending on old ordering)
Verify all tests pass before proceeding
acceptance_criteria: All unit tests pass (cargo nextest run exits 0)
No new failures introduced by the refactor

tasks.name: Dispatch code-reviewer agent for core implementation review
tasks.description: Use superpowers:code-reviewer subagent to review the core change against the plan and the project's coding standards.
steps: Dispatch superpowers:code-reviewer agent with: 'Review the Phase 5 sequential execution refactor in src/phases/local_merge.rs. Original plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-12-sequential-operations.md. Changes to review: apply_consumer_operations function replacing apply_consumer_merges + apply_consumer_filters; execute() now calls single function; existing merges-before-filters test updated. Check for correctness, idiomatic Rust, and that the change matches the plan's intent.'
Address any issues raised by the reviewer (fix code, not just acknowledge)
Re-run tests after any fixes
acceptance_criteria: Code reviewer agent reports no blocking issues
Any raised issues are resolved in code
Tests still pass after review-triggered changes

tasks.name: Add tests for interleaved merge/filter scenarios
tasks.description: Add unit tests covering sequential scenarios that were impossible with the old two-pass approach.
steps: Add test_phase5_sequential_merge_then_exclude (reverse order: merge first, exclude second)
Add test_phase5_sequential_include_merge_exclude (include → merge → exclude interleaved)
Run: cargo nextest run test_phase5_sequential
Expected: All new sequential tests pass
acceptance_criteria: Both new tests exist and pass
See docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-12-sequential-operations.md Tasks 3 and 4 for exact code

tasks.name: Update Phase 5 module and execute() doc comments
tasks.description: Update doc comments to describe the new sequential execution model.
steps: Update module-level doc comment (lines 1-35) to describe single sequential consumer pass
Step 5 in module docs: 'Apply Consumer Operations' (was two steps 5 and 6)
Explain: operations interleave, exclude before merge removes source, include before merge filters FS
Update execute() doc (lines 44-48) to say 'consumer operations in YAML declaration order'
Run: cargo xtask check-prose src/phases/local_merge.rs
Expected: No AI writing patterns detected
acceptance_criteria: Module doc reflects single sequential pass

tasks.name: Commit core implementation and tests
tasks.description: Commit the Phase 5 refactor with tests and doc updates.
steps: Run ./script/ci to verify clean
git add src/phases/local_merge.rs
Commit with conventional message: 'feat: sequential declaration-order execution for Phase 5 consumer operations'
Include BREAKING CHANGE footer describing the new semantics
acceptance_criteria: Message explains the behavior change (merges no longer forced before filters)

tasks.name: Write E2E tests for sequential consumer operations
tasks.description: Add E2E tests exercising sequential ops through the CLI with real git repos as upstreams.
steps: Create tests/cli_e2e_sequential_ops.rs
Add init_test_git_repo helper (copy pattern from tests/cli_e2e_defer.rs:12-56)
Add test_sequential_merge_then_exclude_via_cli
Add test_sequential_rename_then_merge_via_cli
Run: cargo nextest run --test cli_e2e_sequential_ops
Expected: Both tests pass
acceptance_criteria: Tests exercise the full CLI pipeline, not just Phase 5 in isolation

tasks.name: Dispatch review agent to assess E2E test coverage
tasks.description: Use a code-reviewer agent to assess whether E2E tests adequately cover the sequential semantics.
steps: Dispatch superpowers:code-reviewer agent with: 'Review tests/cli_e2e_sequential_ops.rs for E2E coverage of the Phase 5 sequential execution model. The feature change: consumer operations (merges and filters) now execute in YAML declaration order instead of being grouped by type. Assess whether the tests cover the key semantic scenarios: merge-then-filter, filter-then-merge (should error), interleaved ops, and integration with deferred upstream merges. Flag gaps.'
Address any coverage gaps raised (add more tests)
Re-run E2E tests
acceptance_criteria: Review agent reports adequate coverage or gaps are filled

tasks.name: Commit E2E tests
tasks.description: Commit the E2E tests separately from the core change.
steps: Run ./script/ci
git add tests/cli_e2e_sequential_ops.rs
Commit: 'test: add E2E tests for sequential consumer operation execution'

tasks.name: Run full CI and verify no regressions
tasks.description: Final validation that the complete change is clean and ready for PR.
steps: Run: ./script/cibuild
Verify fmt, clippy, all tests (unit + E2E), prose all pass
Run: cargo nextest run --test 'cli_e2e_*' to catch regressions in existing E2E tests
If any issues, fix and commit fixes
acceptance_criteria: No clippy warnings, no prose issues, no formatting issues

tasks.name: Final code review before creating PR
tasks.description: Dispatch a final reviewer to assess the complete branch against the plan.
steps: Dispatch superpowers:code-reviewer agent with: 'Final review of feat/sequential-operations branch. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-12-sequential-operations.md. Assess: does the implementation match the plan? Are commits well-structured? Is the BREAKING CHANGE note accurate? Any edge cases missed?'
Address any final issues
Report readiness to create PR to user

notes: Out of scope (require separate plans): repo-in-sequence resolution, Allium spec updates, detailed/operators.allium
The key diagnostic test is write-key-failing-test - it must FAIL before implement-sequential-pass and PASS after
BREAKING CHANGE: consumers whose configs rely on 'merges always run before filters' must reorder their configs so merges appear before the filters that would remove their source files
Tasks with blocked_by=null can run in parallel (only write-key-failing-test initially)
Update status to complete and last_updated when done
